mnemonics: SSM FORECASTS: State Space Models Formulate Optimal, Reliable, Exact Calculations, Accurately Simulating Trends
keyPoints: State-space models provide a unified framework for forecasting by representing a time series through unobserved state variables and observation equations.
The Kalman filter recursion is used to compute exact finite-sample forecasts and update state estimates as new data becomes available.
This formulation allows for exact calculation of forecast error variances and likelihood functions, which is crucial for model estimation and inference.
commonMistakes: Assuming state-space models are only for linear systems; while the standard Kalman filter is for linear Gaussian models, extensions exist for non-linear cases.
Forgetting to check for stationarity and stability conditions, which are necessary for the Kalman filter to converge and produce reliable forecasts.
Mishandling missing data; while state-space models can accommodate missing observations, improper implementation can lead to biased forecasts.
